name: Tadalafil cognition in healthy adults targeted baseline search

search_queries.query: PubMed tadalafil cognition healthy adults randomized trial
tadalafil cognition humans trial cerebral blood flow study
"Cognitive outcomes after tadalafil treatment in patients with cerebral small vessel disease"
"The effect of phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors on cerebral blood flow in humans"

inclusion_criteria: Human evidence addressing tadalafil, PDE5 inhibitors, cognition, cognitive tests, or brain perfusion relevant to cognition.
Prioritize tadalafil-specific randomized or controlled human studies.
Allow adjacent non-tadalafil PDE5 healthy-adult studies only when no direct tadalafil healthy-adult source is identified and the record clearly marks indirectness.
Include safety or tolerability data when reported in a cognition-relevant tadalafil source.
exclusion_criteria: Animal or in vitro studies for this first healthy-adult cognition baseline.
Commercial, nootropic, or forum pages without inspectable primary source data.
Sources focused only on ED/BPH efficacy without cognitive or neurovascular outcomes.
Case reports or adverse-event sources reserved for the separate safety review unless directly relevant to the cognition baseline.
dedupe_method: Manual deduplication by title, PMID/DOI, and trial name.

screening_decisions.title: Testing the cognitive effects of tadalafil. Neuropsychological secondary outcomes from the PASTIS trial.
screening_decisions.reason: Most directly relevant tadalafil-specific human cognitive outcome paper found for the baseline, though population is older SVD rather than healthy adults.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil Treatment in Patients With Cerebral Small Vessel Disease: The ETLAS-2 Randomized Clinical Trial.
screening_decisions.reason: Tadalafil-specific repeated dosing trial with cognition and adverse-event results; indirect older CSVD population.

screening_decisions.title: The effects of the phosphodiesterase type 5 inhibitor vardenafil on cognitive performance in healthy adults: a behavioral-electroencephalography study.
screening_decisions.reason: Healthy-adult cognition evidence, but adjacent non-tadalafil PDE5 inhibitor; included only to bound class-level claims.

screening_decisions.title: Cognitive outcomes after tadalafil treatment in patients with cerebral small vessel disease: ETLAS-2 sub-study.
screening_decisions.reason: Highly relevant follow-up extraction candidate for a future cognition revision or CSVD-specific scope change; not staged in this first baseline because the main ETLAS-2 paper already supports the broader cognition and tolerability boundary.

screening_decisions.title: The effect of phosphodiesterase-5 inhibitors on cerebral blood flow in humans: A systematic review.
screening_decisions.reason: Useful background for neurovascular mechanism and cerebral blood-flow review, but not direct cognitive outcome evidence for tadalafil in healthy adults.

screening_decisions.title: Tadalafil-induced transient global amnesia.
screening_decisions.reason: Potential safety signal better handled in the dedicated safety/adverse-events review question.

screening_decisions.title: Commercial or nootropic tadalafil cognition pages
screening_decisions.reason: Not primary evidence and often extrapolates from blood-flow mechanisms without source-backed cognitive outcomes.

screening_summary: Targeted searches found no direct human tadalafil cognitive-enhancement trial in healthy men age 30 to 50. The staged baseline therefore uses indirect tadalafil-specific CSVD/SVD trials with null overall cognitive findings plus one adjacent healthy-adult vardenafil study to bound PDE5 class extrapolation.
limitations: Targeted baseline search rather than systematic review.
Web and PubMed queries were optimized for first-pass coverage and may miss unpublished, non-English, or non-indexed studies.
Full text was not checked for the adjacent vardenafil study.
Safety sources were triaged for a separate dedicated safety review.
